2604.04182.title: Comparative reversal learning reveals rigid adaptation in LLMs under non-stationary uncertainty
2604.04182.paper_summary: Non-stationary environments require agents to revise previously learned action values when contingencies change. We treat large language models (LLMs) as sequential decision policies in a two-option probabilistic reversal-learning task with three latent states and switch events triggered by either a performance criterion or timeout. We compare a deterministic fixed transition cycle to a stochastic random schedule that increases volatility, and evaluate DeepSeek-V3.2, Gemini-3, and GPT-5.2, with human data as a behavioural reference. Across models, win-stay was near ceiling while lose-shift was markedly attenuated, revealing asymmetric use of positive versus negative evidence. DeepSeek-V3.2 showed extreme perseveration after reversals and weak acquisition, whereas Gemini-3 and GPT-5.2 adapted more rapidly but still remained less loss-sensitive than humans. Random transitions amplified reversal-specific persistence across LLMs yet did not uniformly reduce total wins, demonstrating that high aggregate payoff can coexist with rigid adaptation. Hierarchical reinforcement-learning (RL) fits indicate dissociable mechanisms: rigidity can arise from weak loss learning, inflated policy determinism, or value polarisation via counterfactual suppression. These results motivate reversal-sensitive diagnostics and volatility-aware models for evaluating LLMs under non-stationary uncertainty.
2604.04182.paper_summary_zh: 非穩定環境要求代理人在情況變化時修正先前學習的行動價值。<lb/>我們將大型語言模型（LLMs）視為在一個具有三個潛在狀態和由表現標準或超時觸發的切換事件的兩選項概率反轉學習任務中的序列決策策略。<lb/>我們將確定性固定轉換循環與增加波動性的隨機隨機計劃進行比較，並評估 DeepSeek-V3.2、Gemini-3 和 GPT-5.2，以人類數據作為行為參考。<lb/>在各模型中，贏留的比例接近上限，而輸轉的比例則顯著減弱，顯示出對正面與負面證據的不對稱使用。<lb/>DeepSeek-V3.2 在反轉後顯示出極端的堅持和弱的獲取能力，而 Gemini-3 和 GPT-5.2 則適應得更快，但仍然對損失的敏感度低於人類。<lb/>隨機轉換在 LLMs 中放大了反轉特定的持久性，但並未均勻降低總贏的次數，顯示出高總體收益可以與僵化的適應共存。<lb/>層級強化學習（RL）擬合表明可分離的機制：僵化可能源自於弱的損失學習、膨脹的政策決定性，或通過反事實抑制造成的價值極化。<lb/>這些結果促使了對反轉敏感的診斷和波動性意識模型的需求，以評估 LLMs 在非穩定不確定性下的表現。
2604.04182.author: Haomiaomiao Wang et.al.
2604.04182.authors: Haomiaomiao Wang, Tomás E Ward, Lili Zhang
